treatment_information.specific_treatment: Cardiac Catheterization with 2 Drug Eluting Stents placed
treatment_information.dosage_or_details: Single DES to RPDA and balloon rescue of the jailed branch of RPL
treatment_information.treatment_response: Patient was stable, chest pain free, and eating after the procedure.

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: Administered as part of the treatment protocol
treatment_information.treatment_response: Patient was stable upon arrival to the floor.
Part of the discharge medication plan to keep the stents open.
Part of the discharge medication plan to keep the stents open.

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: Metoprolol Succinate XL 25 mg PO DAILY

treatment_timeline.event: Patient admitted to the hospital with chest pain.
Underwent cardiac catheterization with placement of 2 Drug Eluting Stents.
Started on Integrilin and given Plavix load.
Patient was stable and eating upon transfer to the floor.
Discharge medications including Aspirin, Plavix, and Metoprolol initiated.

patient_outcome.follow_up_details: Follow-up with cardiologist for medication management.
patient_outcome.long_term_prognosis: Patient will need to take aspirin and Plavix for at least one year.
patient_outcome.quality_of_life: Patient was alert, interactive and cleared for independent activity.
patient_outcome.patient_perspective: Patient informed that chest pain was likely due to unstable angina and not a heart attack.
